password.min: Password must be at least {min} characters
password.minSignIn: Password must be at least 6 characters
password.minSignUp: Password must be at least 8 characters
password.uppercase: Password must contain at least one uppercase letter
password.lowercase: Password must contain at least one lowercase letter
password.number: Password must contain at least one number

confirmPassword.min: Confirm Password must be at least 8 characters

chartName.min: Chart name must be at least 2 characters
chartName.max: Chart name must not exceed 50 characters

databases.min: At least one database must be selected

x-axis.required: X-axis is required. Select a property for the X-axis

y-axis.min: At least one Y-axis is required. Add a property for the Y-axis

joins.invalid: Invalid join configuration. All joins must have source and target properties

public.denied: Chart is not public. To make it public, please change the chart settings and set it to public

featureCards.title: All the tools you need for data visualization

cards.title: Secure
cards.description: Your privacy matters most. We never store your information — only access what you explicitly share.

cards.title: Sorting & Filtering
cards.description: Quickly organize and filter information to surface the most relevant insights.

cards.title: Responsive
cards.description: Charts automatically adjust to fit any device — perfect on phones, tablets, and computers.

cards.title: Dark mode
cards.description: Toggle between light and dark themes instantly, directly within your chart.

cta.title: Unlock your Notion data's potential
cta.description: Stop spending hours on manual charts. Visualize your workspace data instantly with our intuitive builder.
cta.features: Zero code needed • Free account to start

sections.title: Acceptance of Terms
sections.content: By accessing and using this website and the Notion Charts service, you accept and agree to be bound by the terms and provision of this agreement.

sections.title: Use License
sections.content: Permission is granted to temporarily download one copy of the materials (information or software) on Notion Charts for personal, non-commercial transitory viewing only. This is the grant of a license, not a transfer of title, and under this license you may not: modify or copy the materials; use the materials for any commercial purpose or for any public display; attempt to decompile, disassemble, or reverse engineer any software contained on Notion Charts; remove any copyright or other proprietary notations from the materials.

sections.title: Disclaimer
sections.content: The materials on Notion Charts are provided on an 'as is' basis. Notion Charts makes no warranties, expressed or implied, and hereby disclaims and negates all other warranties including, without limitation, implied warranties or conditions of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement of intellectual property or other violation of rights.

sections.title: Limitations
sections.content: In no event shall Notion Charts or its suppliers be liable for any damages (including, without limitation, damages for loss of data or profit, or due to business interruption) arising out of the use or inability to use the materials on Notion Charts.

sections.title: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at support@notionchart.com.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.content: We collect information you provide directly to us, such as when you create an account, connect your Notion workspace, or contact us for support. This may include your name, email address, and information about your Notion workspace.

sections.title: How We Use Your Information
sections.content: We use the information we collect to provide, maintain, and improve our services. We will not sell or rent your personal information to third parties. Your Notion data is never stored on our servers – we only access it temporarily to generate your charts.

sections.title: Data Security
sections.content: We take reasonable measures to protect your personal information. However, no security system is impenetrable. We cannot guarantee the absolute security of your information.

sections.title: Your Rights
sections.content: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal information at any time. You can disconnect your Notion workspace and request deletion of your account data.

sections.title: Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at privacy@notionchart.com.

forbidden.title: Forbidden
forbidden.description: This action or resource is forbidden. If you believe this is a mistake, please contact us.

signup.description: Enter your email below to create your account

verifyEmail.description: We've sent you a confirmation email. Please check your inbox and click the link to verify your account.

verifyEmail.resendError: Failed to send verification email. Please try again.

new.title: Create New Chart
new.description: Fill out the form to create a new chart from your Notion data.

delete.title: Delete Chart
delete.description: Are you sure you want to delete this chart? This action cannot be undone.

share.title: Share chart
share.description: Anyone with this link can view the data displayed on the chart.
share.makePublicDescription: Only public charts can be embedded and viewed without authorization.

delete.title: Delete Connection
delete.description: Are you sure you want to delete this connection? This action cannot be undone. Also, remember to disconnect the integration in Notion Settings.

emptyState.title: Connect your Notion
emptyState.description: Connect your Notion to start creating charts from your data. You decide which pages we can access – we handle the rest.

emptyCharts.title: You don't have any charts yet
emptyCharts.description: Create your first chart to visualize your Notion data. It's quick and easy!

dropCharts.title: Time to add a chart to your dashboard
dropCharts.description: Just drag a chart from the sidebar into this area

limit.title: Limit
limit.dataLimitTooltip: Maximum number of data rows to display on the chart

welcome.title: Welcome to Dashboard!
welcome.content: Let's learn how to add charts to your dashboard using drag & drop. It's simple and intuitive!

sidebarCharts.title: Your Charts
sidebarCharts.content: All your charts are located here in the sidebar. You can easily add them to your dashboard.

grabChart.title: Grab a Chart
grabChart.content: Hover over a chart and hold down the left mouse button. Then drag the chart onto the dashboard.

dropZone.title: Drop It Here
dropZone.content: Drag the chart to any part of the dashboard and release the mouse button. The chart will automatically position itself in the grid.

resizeChart.title: Resize Charts
resizeChart.content: Hover over the bottom-right corner of a chart – an icon will appear. Click and drag to resize the chart.

moveChart.title: Move Charts Around
moveChart.content: You can also drag charts within the dashboard to rearrange them. Just click and move!

complete.title: All Done!
complete.content: Now you know how to work with charts on your dashboard. Create, drag, and customize your perfect dashboard!

welcome.title: Welcome to your first chart!
welcome.content: Let's look at how to customize it so it looks exactly how you want

settingsAccordion.title: Chart Settings
settingsAccordion.content: Click to open. Here you can change the chart name, type and theme

settingsName.title: Chart Name
settingsName.content: Enter a descriptive name so you can easily identify the chart among others

settingsType.title: Chart Type
settingsType.content: Select the chart type that best displays your data (bar, line, pie, etc.)

settingsTheme.title: Theme
settingsTheme.content: Select a color scheme that matches your style or brand. You can change it at any time

filtersAccordion.title: Filters
filtersAccordion.content: Click to open. Here you can create and edit filters to focus on specific data or exclude noise

filtersAction.title: Create Filter
filtersAction.content: Click to add your first filter. You can combine multiple conditions using AND/OR for more complex logic

axisAccordion.title: Axes
axisAccordion.content: Click to open. Here you select which fields to use for the X and Y axes, and you can also add multiple Y axes to compare different metrics on the same chart

axisX.title: X Axis
axisX.content: Select a property for the X axis. Usually this is a category or time interval by which you want to distribute your data

axisY.title: Y Axis
axisY.content: Select a property for the Y axis. This is a numeric field that you want to measure or compare on the chart. The Y axis can be omitted, in which case the chart will show the count of records for each category on the X axis, or you can select one or more numeric fields for more detailed analysis

axisAdd.title: Add Axis
axisAdd.content: Adds another Y axis so you can compare multiple metrics on the same chart. This is useful for identifying relationships between different metrics

axisRemove.title: Remove Axis
axisRemove.content: Removes an extra Y axis from the chart

cacheAccordion.title: Caching
cacheAccordion.content: Click to open. Here you can configure how long the chart will cache data for faster loading. A shorter cache duration means the chart will update more frequently from Notion, but may load slower. A longer cache duration provides instant loading, but data may be less up-to-date

cacheDuration.title: Cache Duration
cacheDuration.content: Less is more up-to-date, more is faster.

sortAccordion.title: Sorting
sortAccordion.content: Click to open. Set the order in which data is displayed on the chart by selecting a field to sort by and a direction (ascending or descending)

sortProperty.title: Sort By
sortProperty.content: Select a property by which you want to sort the data on the chart. This can be any field from your Notion database that makes sense for organizing your data (e.g., date, numeric value, or text)

sortDirection.title: Direction
sortDirection.content: Choose the sort direction: ascending (from smallest to largest) or descending (from largest to smallest)

limitAccordion.title: Limit
limitAccordion.content: Click to open. Here you can set the maximum number of data rows that will be displayed on the chart. This is useful if you have a very large database and want to focus only on the most important or most recent records

limitValue.title: Limit Rows
limitValue.content: Enter the maximum number of data rows you want to display on the chart. Leave empty to show all data, but be aware that this may affect chart loading speed if you have a very large database

complete.title: Complete
complete.content: Now you know how to set up your chart so it looks and works exactly the way you want. Don't be afraid to experiment with different settings to find the perfect representation of your data!
